ink-muted.note: Corrected from #999999 (light) / #777777 (dark), which failed WCAG AA (as low as 2.61:1) on card and screen backgrounds. New values (#6c6c6c light, #888888 dark) clear 4.5:1 against both.

header.purpose: Screen title only, one per screen.

amount.purpose: The hero dollar/percent figure inside a card.

label.purpose: Badges, status pills, edge/leg detail rows.

shadows.name: card-rest
shadows.purpose: Ambient lift for every card-shaped component, exported as `cardShadow` from constants/Shadow.ts. Uses RN's unified boxShadow string (RN 0.86+), not the deprecated discrete shadow* props. Live on GameCard and ParlayCard (Tonight tab); still pending on the four budget-tab card components.

components.name: Budget Period Card
components.description: Primary allowance card: category title, status badge, spent/limit amount, progress track.
components.html:
Betting82% used
$410 of $500 spent · $90 left
components.css: .ds-card { background:#f5f5f5; border:0.5px solid #e2e2e2; border-radius:12px; padding:16px; } .ds-card-header { display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:space-between; margin-bottom:8px; } .ds-card-title { font-size:14px; font-weight:500; color:#000; } .ds-badge { padding:3px 8px; border-radius:6px; font-size:12px; font-weight:500; } .ds-badge-warning { background:#faeeda; color:#ba7517; } .ds-amount-row { display:flex; align-items:baseline; margin-bottom:8px; } .ds-amount { font-size:22px; font-weight:500; color:#000; } .ds-amount-sub { font-size:13px; color:#666; } .ds-track { height:6px; border-radius:4px; overflow:hidden; background:#e2e2e2; } .ds-track-fill { height:100%; border-radius:4px; background:#ba7517; }

components.name: Status Badge (ok/warning/danger)
components.description: Filled read-only status pill reused for budget %, bet outcome, and category state.
components.css: .ds-badge { padding:3px 8px; border-radius:6px; font-size:12px; font-weight:500; display:inline-block; } .ds-badge-ok { background:#e1f5ee; color:#1d9e75; } .ds-badge-warning { background:#faeeda; color:#ba7517; } .ds-badge-danger { background:#fcebeb; color:#a32d2d; }

components.name: Category Tile
components.description: Compact side-by-side tile for a non-betting budget category, two per row.
components.css: .ds-tile { flex:1; background:#f5f5f5; border-radius:8px; padding:16px; } .ds-tile-label { display:block; font-size:13px; color:#666; margin-bottom:4px; } .ds-tile-amount { font-size:20px; font-weight:500; color:#000; } .ds-tile-amount-sub { font-size:13px; font-weight:400; color:#666; }

components.name: Log a Bet Button
components.description: Outline trigger button on the Recent Bets card header; opens the quick-entry modal. Deliberately quiet — the modal's own submit button is where the accent commits.
components.css: .ds-btn-log { background:transparent; border:0.5px solid #e2e2e2; border-radius:8px; padding:6px 12px; font-size:13px; color:#000; transition:opacity 0.15s; } .ds-btn-log:hover { opacity:0.7; } .ds-btn-log:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #873273; outline-offset:2px; } .ds-btn-log:active { opacity:0.5; }

components.name: Log Bet Submit Button
components.description: The one filled, full-commitment button in the system — modal's final submit action, plum fill, white text.
components.css: .ds-btn-submit { background:#873273; color:#fff; border:none; border-radius:8px; padding:14px; font-size:15px; font-weight:500; text-align:center; transition:background 0.15s; } .ds-btn-submit:hover { background:#70125d; } .ds-btn-submit:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #873273; outline-offset:2px; } .ds-btn-submit:active { background:#70125d; }

components.name: Game Card (Tonight tab)
components.description: Compact matchup card with status badge and a list of positive-edge legs from playstat.
components.html:
Yankees @ Red SoxUpcoming
J. Smith over 1.5 total_bases (+120)
components.css: .ds-game-card { background:#f5f5f5; border:0.5px solid #e2e2e2; border-radius:12px; padding:14px; box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.06); } .ds-game-header { display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:space-between; gap:8px; } .ds-game-matchup { font-size:14px; font-weight:500; flex:1; } .ds-game-badge { padding:3px 8px; border-radius:6px; font-size:11px; font-weight:500; background:#e2e2e2; color:#873273; } .ds-edge-row { font-size:12px; color:#666; margin-top:8px; } .ds-edge-odds { color:#873273; }

narrative.overview:
Budgerr App reads like a plainspoken accounting notebook, not a fintech dashboard. Every number — grocery spend, bank cash flow, a bet's stake — gets the same flat card, the same 500-weight amount, the same hairline border. There is no separate "fun" visual register for the betting category; the Quiet Ledger doesn't celebrate a parlay any louder than it reports a grocery bill, because the whole point of this app is that a bet is just another line, not a special event.

The system rejects the two things it's easiest to drift toward: generic navy-and-teal SaaS-finance chrome (stacked gradient-metric cards, hero KPI tiles), and sportsbook/casino energy (neon odds badges, celebratory color). A muted plum accent (#873273) replaces the unmodified Expo-template blue this system launched with — plum reads as a personal, warm choice rather than corporate-trustworthy blue, and its hue sits far enough from the success/warning/danger set that it can never be mistaken for a status color. Card Rest, the system's one ambient shadow, has landed on the Tonight tab's cards as the first application of the "tactile and confident" direction; the four budget-tab cards still need the same treatment.
keyCharacteristics: Flat, hairline-bordered cards as the one repeating container — no card-inside-card nesting anywhere in the codebase today
A single three-way status vocabulary (success/warning/danger) reused identically across budget bars, bet outcomes, and category badges
System font only, small type scale (11–22px), weight doing the hierarchy work instead of size jumps
Restrained color: one deliberate plum accent for actions/selection/emphasis, plus the semantic success/warning/danger set — nothing decorative

rules.name: The One Ledger Rule
rules.body: A dollar amount, a percentage, and a bet outcome share the same three status colors and the same type scale. Never introduce a fourth color or a bigger number treatment just because the row happens to be about betting.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Quiet Plum is the only non-semantic color in the system. If a new component wants "brand personality," reach for plum at low dosage (an icon tint, a selected state), never a second accent hue.

rules.name: The Weight-Not-Size Rule
rules.body: Emphasis is 400→500 weight, not a bigger font. Nothing on any screen jumps more than ~8px between adjacent hierarchy levels.

rules.name: The One Shadow Rule
rules.body: There is exactly one shadow in this system, used identically on every card. If a component needs to feel "more important," raise its type weight or add a status color, not a heavier shadow.

dos: Do keep every dollar amount, percentage, and bet outcome inside the same three-color status vocabulary (success/warning/danger) — one meaning, one set of colors, everywhere.
Do route any new semantic color through constants/Colors.ts, light and dark variants both, before using it in a component.
Do reserve accent (Quiet Plum) for primary action, current selection, and interactive emphasis — never decoration, never a second brand color alongside it.
Do keep type hierarchy to weight changes (400/500) within the existing 11–22px range rather than introducing a larger display size.
Do apply the single Card Rest shadow via the shared cardShadow import from constants/Shadow.ts — no per-component shadow tuning, no redeclaring the values inline.
Do pair every status color with a label or icon, never color alone, per PRODUCT.md's accessibility requirement.
donts: Don't introduce a navy-and-teal or gradient-metric "generic fintech" look — no gradient fills, no stacked hero-KPI cards, no Mint/Copilot-style dashboard chrome.
Don't give the betting category louder, brighter, or more celebratory styling than any other budget category — no neon badges, no sportsbook-style odds chips, no confetti.

Don't hardcode a new color directly in a component's StyleSheet — route it through constants/Colors.ts first, the way edge/edgeBg/edgeBorder now replace the emerald ParlayCard/GameCard used to hardcode.
Don't reach for blue as a second accent, or as a fallback if plum ever feels "too bold" — blue is the generic-fintech default this system explicitly moved away from.
Don't stack more than one shadow value on a card, or add a shadow to a badge/pill — Card Rest is a card-only treatment.
